summary: Move the privileged roles of a deployment from the deployer EOA to a Safe, using the grant-new-before-revoke-old ceremony. The authoritative ordering and the pre-revoke gate are in roles.md.

inputs.safe: the recipient Safe address (deploy one with the deploy-safe step if absent)
inputs.mode: safe (the accepts execute from the Safe)

steps.successCriteria: a Safe is deployed at a deterministic address
the TokenAdminRegistry pendingAdministrator is the Safe
the pool ownership transfer to the Safe is initiated (two-step)
rateLimitAdmin and feeAdmin are the Safe (router preserved)
the TokenAdminRegistry administrator is the Safe
the pool owner is the Safe

the covered roles show pending or accepted to the Safe. The deployer EOA is still a residual holder (expected at this point), so roles-check does NOT yet exit 0. Do NOT revoke to force a clean check: the revoke (batch C), the pre-revoke gate, and the DENY completion sweep are in roles.md and must run in that order.

coverageNote: This skeleton covers the TAR administrator, pool owner, rateLimitAdmin, and feeAdmin only. The full role surface (the token top-level admin via TransferTokenAdmin, SetCCIPAdmin / burnMintAdmin, lockbox and hooks owners, and the v1 LockRelease rebalancer) is enumerated in roles.md's step-0 declaration. Manifest-complete is NOT handoff-complete: reconcile against roles.md, not this subset.
ceremonyNote: This manifest is the machine skeleton and deliberately contains NO revoke step. The authoritative grant-before-revoke ordering, the pre-revoke gate (roles-check must exit 0 for the pending state before any revoke), the token-admin grant/accept per template, and the residual-holder DENY sweep are in roles.md; do not add or reorder revokes from this skeleton alone.
